compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G3 container coverage review (parent 3fe1 + this logging-substrate child): clean — no findings.

This child fully and uniquely covers parent AC1 (the logging substrate exists — named loggers + NullHandler in src/rebar/__init__.py + stderr StreamHandler at BOTH entrypoints (CLI + MCP main) — AND a stdout-purity contract test guarding the reconciler json.loads(subprocess stdout) contract at reconcile.py:472). It also materially contributes to SC2 (stdout stays pure / CLI data contract + MCP framing intact) by standing up the stderr handlers and the purity guard that later print->logging migrations rely on.

4-bucket map vs parent criteria for THIS pairing:
- AC1 (substrate + stdout-purity test): FULLY by this child.
- AC2 (exemplar + enable BLE001/T201 + shrinking allowlist): structural — covered by sibling 16a1.
- AC3 (~100 unguarded sites in batches): structural — covered by sweeps 8d01/d360/ceaa/fd72/0569 (this child explicitly changes no except body).
- AC4 (narrowing surfaced bug + regression test): structural — covered by 16a1/ceaa + sweeps.
- AC5 (allowlist emptied + final gate): structural — covered by 0ec1.
- SC1 (one lint-enforced way): structural — 16a1 + 0ec1.
- SC2 (failures observable on stderr logger; KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit never swallowed; stdout pure): partial — this child provides the stderr-logger substrate + stdout-purity guard; the swallow-observability and signal-safety pieces land in the sweeps.
- SC3 (future audit finds no unguarded cluster; new offenders fail CI): structural — 16a1 enables the gate, 0ec1 finalizes.

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered when checked against the whole sibling roster, and no coverage gap is attributable to this pairing. The "lands FIRST / no except changes" scoping is consistent with the parent (substrate is the prerequisite for logging swallowed exceptions).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this logging-substrate child; whole sibling roster considered). CLEAN — empty findings list. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked; each is benign at high confidence.

This child is the foundational substrate: it stands up named loggers + a `rebar`-root `NullHandler` in `src/rebar/__init__.py`, installs a stderr `StreamHandler` at BOTH entrypoints, and adds a stdout-purity contract test. Crucially it changes NO `except` body and does NOT author the allowlist (that is 16a1) — so it is a pure plumbing/observability-floor enabler that lands first.

Mode-by-mode (verified against the codebase):

- Implicit shared state: clean. This child does not author or mutate the BLE001/T201 per-file-ignores allowlist (that is implicit shared state owned by 16a1 → sweeps → 0ec1). It only adds the NullHandler + entrypoint handlers; no shared mutable state is contended here.

- Conflicting assumptions: clean. Entrypoint targets verified to exist exactly as the ACs claim — `src/rebar/cli.py:18 main`, `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:318 main`, `src/rebar/mcp_server.py:901 main` (console scripts `rebar` / `rebar-mcp = rebar.mcp_server:main` in pyproject.toml). The "install a stderr handler at MCP main, never stdout" assumption is consistent with the MCP sweep (ceaa: "keep the server off stdout"). The reconciler runs as a separate subprocess (`python -m rebar_reconciler`) with its own stderr prints (owned by 0569), so no handler-namespace conflict is introduced here.

- Dependency gap: clean for THIS pairing. The child's ACs are self-contained (NullHandler + handlers + purity test). The `_io/` un-owned-package gap and the scoped-T201/reconciler exemption gap are real roster holes, but they are NOT attributable to this pairing — this child neither authors the allowlist nor enables BLE001/T201. Those are already filed against 16a1 (allowlist author) and 0ec1 (allowlist-empty gate); not re-attributed here per the absence cross-check.

- Scope overlap: clean (benign). `src/rebar/__init__.py` is in both this child's and sweep 8d01's file_impact, and `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` is in both this child's and sweep ceaa's. The edits are DISTINCT and non-conflicting: this child adds the NullHandler / handler-install (explicitly "no except changes"), while 8d01 does the `_json_or` narrow at `__init__.py:228` and ceaa does the MCP raise/ToolError remediation. Correctly ordered (8fbd blocks 16a1; 16a1 blocks 8d01/ceaa), so this child lands its plumbing edit before the sweeps touch the except bodies. 8d01's own G4 review already classified this overlap as benign.

- Ordering violation: clean. "Lands FIRST / no except changes" is correctly encoded as 8fbd → 16a1 → all sweeps. The substrate must precede any swallow-logging (you cannot log into a handler that does not exist), and the dep chain enforces it.

- Consumer impact: clean. The stdout-purity test guards the reconciler's `json.loads(subprocess stdout)` contract — verified at `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:466`, which runs `[cli, "list", "--full"]` and `json.loads(result.stdout)`. The child's test (`rebar list --full` stdout parses as JSON, diagnostics on stderr) precisely guards this exact call; it protects the consumer rather than regressing it.

- Residual references: clean. The only drift is a stale line citation: parent + this child cite `reconcile.py:472` for the `json.loads(stdout)` contract, but the actual call is at `reconcile.py:466`. Benign documentation noise (points at the same load-bearing call, no dangling code reference) — below the high-confidence / benign-reading bar.

No high-confidence cross-child contradiction, scope conflict, or ordering gap is attributable to this (parent + logging-substrate child) pairing. Clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Substrate landed (commit 232b01c9). New rebar._logging module (convention reference docstring + idempotent install_stderr_handler honouring REBAR_LOG_LEVEL, stderr-only). NullHandler on rebar root (__init__.py) and sibling rebar_reconciler root. Stderr StreamHandler installed in all three entrypoint mains (CLI, MCP, reconciler subprocess). stdout-purity contract test added (list --full parses as JSON; forced diagnostic to stderr, stdout stays pure JSON). No except body changed. Full suite: 3201 passed/12 skipped; ruff clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS** — all 6 acceptance criteria are met.

Evidence gathered:

1. **`rebar` root NullHandler** (`src/rebar/__init__.py:29`): `logging.getLogger("rebar").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` — confirmed.

2. **CLI stderr handler** (`src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:328–330`): `install_stderr_handler("rebar")` called inside `main()`. `src/rebar/cli.py:main()` delegates to this via `_main(sys.argv[1:])` — confirmed.

3. **MCP stderr handler** (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py:901–907`): `main()` calls `install_stderr_handler("rebar")` before `build_server().run()` — confirmed. Handler targets `sys.stderr` (`_logging.py:81`), never stdout.

4. **Reconciler subprocess stderr handler + `rebar_reconciler` NullHandler**: `__main__.py:280–282` calls `install_stderr_handler("rebar_reconciler")`; `__init__.py:12` has `logging.getLogger("rebar_reconciler").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` — both confirmed.

5. **Stdout-purity contract test** (`tests/integration/test_stdout_purity.py`): two tests present — `test_list_full_stdout_is_pure_json` (runs `rebar list --full` and asserts `json.loads(stdout)`) and `test_diagnostic_goes_to_stderr_not_stdout` (forces a `logger.warning(exc_info=True)`, asserts diagnostic on stderr and stdout remains pure JSON) — confirmed.

6. **Convention docstring in `_logging.py`**: the module docstring is the full convention reference (named loggers, NullHandler hygiene, entrypoint handler install, stderr-only, observability floor, BLE001/T201 enforcement) — confirmed. No `except` body changes detected in any patched file.

compiled_state.description:
# Logging substrate: named loggers + NullHandler + stderr entrypoint handlers + stdout-purity test

Foundational, lands FIRST — you cannot log a swallowed exception into a handler that does not exist, and
the print->logging migration depends on this. No `except` changes in this ticket (zero behavior change to
error handling); this only stands up the logging plumbing and the contract guard.

## What
- Establish the named-logger convention: modules use `logging.getLogger(__name__)` under the `rebar` root.
- Add `logging.getLogger("rebar").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` in `src/rebar/__init__.py` (library
  hygiene — quiet by default).
- Install a **stderr** `StreamHandler` at ALL THREE entrypoints: the CLI `main` (`src/rebar/cli.py` /
  `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`), the MCP `main` (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py:main`), AND the reconciler
  subprocess `main` (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:main`). Never target stdout.
- **Reconciler logger namespace (plan-review G4):** the reconciler is a THIRD subprocess entrypoint and its
  modules use `logging.getLogger(__name__)`, so its loggers resolve under the **sibling** root
  `rebar_reconciler.*`, NOT under `rebar`. A `NullHandler` on the `rebar` root does NOT cover it. So also add
  `logging.getLogger("rebar_reconciler").addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` in
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__init__.py` and wire the stderr handler in that subprocess's `main`,
  or diagnostics migrated there (sweep `colder-bugle-rake`) are dropped/last-resort-formatted — inverting SC2.
- Add a stdout-purity contract test: `rebar list --full` stdout is pure parseable JSON and diagnostics go
  to stderr (guards the reconciler's `json.loads(subprocess stdout)` contract at `reconcile.py:472`).
- Document the convention in the logging module's docstring (this docstring IS the convention reference).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/__init__.py` adds a `NullHandler` to the `rebar` root logger.
- [ ] CLI entry (`src/rebar/cli.py` / `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`) installs a stderr `StreamHandler`.
- [ ] MCP entry (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py` `main`) installs a stderr `StreamHandler` (never stdout).
- [ ] Reconciler subprocess entry (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py` `main`) installs a stderr
      `StreamHandler`, and `_engine/rebar_reconciler/__init__.py` adds a `NullHandler` on the
      `rebar_reconciler` root logger (it is a sibling of `rebar`, not under it).
- [ ] A new test asserts `rebar list --full` stdout parses as JSON and a forced diagnostic appears on stderr.
- [ ] No `except` body is changed; `make lint` + full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: new logging substrate module + convention reference docstring
install stderr handler in reconciler subprocess main
install stderr handler in MCP main
install stderr handler in CLI main
